There's a fine line between genius and insanity, and it's difficult to say on what side of that line Animators reside. These fanatical magi spend their days perfecting their Animatus creations: unique creatures that grow more powerful as their master's dark experiments unveil new opportunities.


As an Animator, you will always have a unique creature in your party called an Animatus. Your entire battle strategy will likely focus on this creature. You can empower your Animatus, and even use your other creatures to make it stronger in combat.


(You can, in theory, Not have Animatus on your team. But why would you?)

Relics of the past hold secrets lost to time, and the Antiquarian is determined to uncover them all. This eccentric magi studies the ancient artifacts left behind by the gods themselves, empowering her creatures with forgotten blessings that even the gods have long since forsaken.


As an Antiquarian, you'll specialize in enhancing your creatures' Relics, unlocking hidden abilites that are unique to this specialization. Your creatures' Relics will gain new effects that no other specialization can access, allowing you to customize your team's playstyle in ways that go far beyond the ordinary.

The Blood Mage is a deranged spellcaster who is willing to do anything to overcome his foes - even at the cost of his own creatures' lives. This menacing spellcaster is adept at manipulating the lifeforce of allies and foes alike in his endless pursuit for power.


As a Blood Mage, your creatures will grow more powerful as they take damage. To facilitate these effects, the Blood Mage offers a variety of different ways to inflict damage on his own creatures.

Strategy
[
edit
]


 
Bloodmage
 is one of the easier specs to build for: you will clobber enemies with your healthbar, but otherwise won't have to jump through extra hoops. Look for traits that interact with Maximum and Current 
Health
, such as 
Brownie Brute
's Ultimatum. 
Giant
, 
Koloss
, 
Maniac
 species will serve the bloodmage well. In the late game, 
Avatar of Genaros
 will help 
 
Bloodmage
's team scale to a ridiculous level. 

Especially in the early game, it is easier to stack Maximum Health to take advantage of 
 
Bloodrage
 and 
 
Voracity
 rather than trying to keep your health low to take advantage of the missing Health traits such as 
 
Endless Slaughter
 and 
 
March Towards Death
, which keep you very close to death.

Keep in mind that while 
Health
 scales your damage, you still need 
Speed
 lest enemies just dodge your attacks.

Cabalists can conjure Spell Gems out of thin air to be used by their creatures at any given moment. These magi prefer to employ high-risk, high-reward battle strategies to ensure their victory.

Many people foolishly mistake a Druid's introversion for weakness. These magi believe that quality always trumps quantity on the battlefield. Contrary to tradition, they are often seen with fewer than six creatures at their disposal, but the havoc their limited numbers wreak when augmented by Druidic magic is a sight to behold.


As a Druid, you'll probably only want to have one (or up to three, later on) creatures in your party. Your creatures will gain massive benefits for being part of a smaller party.

(cited from gay moth aunt's spec guide)

 
Purgatorian
 is a 
very
 high-risk class, since the class's perks rely on most of your team being dead - and thus your enemies will have fewer targets to kill. 
 
Designated Survivor
 and 
 
Masochism
 can blunt this, you should still prepare your team build very carefully to allow your final creature to stay standing.

While your dead creatures can still act on your behalf and more strongly than they would while alive, and are effectively beyond retaliation, they still act randomly which gives you less ability to focus down priority targets.

There are a handful of existing traits that allow creatures to act while dead that could synergize with this specialization - in addition, revival-oriented creatures such as 
 
Wight
s and 
 
Phoenix
es may be of high value to a 
 
Purgatorian
 looking for perk synergies.

Strategy
[
edit
]


You want creatures and spells that inflict 
Confused
, force enemies to 
Attack
, and increase damage they deal to each other (like 
Emerald Paragon
) - and some protection in case aftershocks of them fighting each other reach your creatures (for example, 
Phase Knight
). It's a good idea to keep an option to actually do damage yourself in case of enemy 
Nature Shapeshifter
 shutting down your whole gimmick, or last standing enemy being too weak to slay itself.

Post-storyline trait item 
Sarea's Broken Heart
 will make enemies attack each other at the 
start of battle
, which doubles down on 
 
Witch Doctor
's strengths.
